claim: Smc5/6 engagment drives the activation of SUMO E3 ligase Mms21 by ATP-dependent remolding.

result.claim: Smc5/6 engagment drives the activation of SUMO E3 ligase Mms21 by ATP-dependent remolding.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper ID 17628888, "ATPase-Dependent Control of the Mms21 SUMO Ligase during DNA Repair." This paper explicitly states that "Mms21-dependent sumoylation requires binding of ATP to Smc5" (prop_2712) and that "the Smc5-Mms21 heterodimer is physically remodeled in an ATP-dependent manner" (prop_2715, prop_2713). Furthermore, it highlights that "the ATP-binding activity of the Smc5/6 complex is coordinated with its SUMO ligase" (prop_2716) and that this coordination occurs "through the coiled coil domain of Smc5" (prop_2718). The abstract of the paper also confirms these findings, stating that "Mms21-dependent sumoylation requires binding of ATP to Smc5" and that "scanning force microscopy of the Smc5-Mms21 heterodimer shows that the molecule is physically remodeled in an ATP-dependent manner." While the evidence is direct and consistent, the paper's credibility is somewhat limited as it has 0 citations and an unknown publication year.
